title: Pan-Seared Roast Greek Beef Roast
description: A yeasty, crisp Greek dish featuring gelatine leaves, roast, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is anti-inflammatory-friendly and served in a beautiful lacquerware bowl.

ingredients.name: warm water
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Use warm, not hot water.

instructions: Rinse the beef roast and pat dry with paper towels.
In a large bowl, combine yeast, warm water, and 1 tsp sugar. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes, until foamy.
Add the olive oil, onion, garlic, lemon zest, olives, oregano, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the bowl. Mix well.
Add the gelatine leaves to the bowl and mix until they are fully incorporated.
Place the beef roast in the bowl and coat evenly with the yeast mixture.
Put the beef roast in a lacquerware bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
Place the bowl in the oven and roast for 20 minutes.
Continue roasting for another 20-25 minutes, until the roast reaches your desired level of doneness.
Let the roast rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Optional: Crumble 1/4 cup of feta cheese over the top of the roast.
Garnish with fresh oregano and serve immediately.
Season with sea salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot in the lacquerware bowl.
Use a meat thermometer to ensure the roast reaches a safe internal temperature of 145°F for medium-rare.
Reheat the roast in the oven at 300°F for 5-10 minutes, if desired.
Serve with your choice of sides, such as roasted vegetables or a side salad.
